verte.title: Absinthe
verte.subtitle: Nowhere's End NE-01
verte.intro: The flagship verte: alpine, bitter, louche-friendly, and deliberately classical in silhouette.
verte.story: This SKU is built as the house reference point. It starts with a traditional wormwood-anise-fennel trinity, then widens with green botanicals so the finish stays high and herbaceous rather than syrupy.

recipeSections.title: Primary botanical bill
items: Sets the bitter spine and the sovereign absinthe signature.
Carries the sweet anisic core and louche behavior.
Rounds the mid-palate and softens anise sharpness.
Adds alpine lift and green medicinal structure.
Pushes the profile toward fresh citron-green rather than confection.
Adds nuance and soft wormwood echo in the finish.

recipeSections.title: SKU cues
items: The appearance should read naturally green rather than artificially saturated.
Anisic sweetness remains present but is restrained by bitter lift.
The last impression should be wormwood, fennel, and cool resin.

herbs: sweet aromatic spine and louche support
resinous green top notes and classic color contribution
fresh citron lift without breaking the style
molecules: sweet anisic body and louche behavior
dry fennel tension that keeps the sweetness trimmed
small floral lift that broadens the mid-palate
visual identity in the colored SKU
serviceNotes: Best framed as the reference bottle that defines the family grammar.
Works when the narrative is historical continuity rather than spectacle.

rouge.title: Crimson
rouge.subtitle: Nowhere's End NE-02
rouge.intro: A rouge SKU that keeps absinthe recognizable while letting hibiscus and rose push the line toward garnet and bloom.
rouge.story: Crimson is not a liqueur masquerading as absinthe. It stays rooted in wormwood and anise, then bends toward hibiscus for tart color, rose for perfume, and melissa for freshness.

recipeSections.title: Primary botanical bill
items: Maintains the bitter axis so the SKU remains anchored.
Preserves absinthe identity and louche structure.
Renders the red build softer and more continuous.
Supplies tartness, anthocyanin color, and red-fruit illusion.
Adds perfume and a soft floral bridge over the hibiscus acid line.
Keeps the nose lifted and avoids syrupy collapse.

recipeSections.title: SKU cues
items: The bottle should read like stained glass rather than opaque syrup.
The red system should sharpen the absinthe spine without overwhelming it.
Rose and wormwood should remain after the hibiscus flash fades.

herbs: provides tartness, color, and red-fruit illusion
gives the floral bridge and perfume
keeps the top note lively and not jammy
molecules: stops the profile from becoming plush
adds rose bloom and soft lift
serviceNotes: Best treated as the dramatic bottle in the line without losing the core identity.
Useful wherever the visual story matters as much as the louche.

detour.title: Detour
detour.subtitle: Nowhere's End NE-03
detour.intro: The family rebel: an absinthe-adjacent SKU that steps away from licorice toward lemon herbs, cool bitters, and blue-purple theater.
detour.story: Detour deliberately breaks the expectation that absinthe must always read as anise first. Lemon balm and lemon verbena lead, butterfly pea handles the chromatic shift, and softer bitter herbs keep it from becoming just a tea cordial.

recipeSections.title: Primary botanical bill
items: Acts as the aromatic signature and the green-citrus center.
Creates the blue visual system and pH-responsive color play.
Keeps an absinthe echo without hard licorice weight.
Adds cool herbal altitude and medicinal tension.
Pushes the profile toward leaf-citrus precision.
Anchors the top notes and gives the SKU structure.

recipeSections.title: SKU cues
items: The chromatic story is core to the product identity.
This SKU should feel botanical and exploratory rather than candy-like.
The final impression should be green, cooling, and strange in a good way.

herbs: primary aromatic signature and green-citrus lift
blue chromatic source and theatrical shift
keeps an absinthe echo without heavy licorice
molecules: adds melissa depth and cooling herbal persistence
keeps the top note polished instead of raw
keeps the profile airy rather than sweet
serviceNotes: This is the SKU for color ritual and deliberate deviation.
Its success depends on the balance between curiosity and coherence.

bleue.title: Chloris
bleue.subtitle: Nowhere's End NE-04
bleue.intro: The clear bottle in the line: all structure, no green coloration, with a cleaner and more architectural profile.
bleue.story: Chloris strips away the colored-herb halo and lets the base grammar appear in clear form. It still speaks absinthe, but it does so with sharper lines and less visual camouflage.

recipeSections.title: Primary botanical bill
items: Retains louche and the expected absinthe center.
Rounds and supports the anisic line.
Adds citrus-spice lift and dries the finish.
Acts as fixative structure and body.
Adds gentler bitters without adding green color expectation.

recipeSections.title: SKU cues
items: Clear in bottle, opaline in louche
Transparency is part of the SKU's confidence.
This SKU should feel cleaner and more technical than the verte.
Coriander and angelica should make the exit more skeletal and elegant.

herbs: adds optional nuance without green coloration
molecules: holds the louche and sweet spice line
serviceNotes: This is the SKU for clarity, not spectacle.
It should feel deliberate, transparent, and composed.
